claims.text: Employees ask our internal knowledge base more than 15,000 questions every day
One Postgres table holds embeddings, LLM-generated summaries, and metadata from every source
The RRF formula traces to Cormack, Clarke, and Büttcher's 2009 SIGIR paper, and k=60 is genuinely that paper's own recommended constant
Both Elasticsearch and OpenSearch ship RRF with k=60 as their own default
A peer-reviewed 2022 critique in ACM TOIS shows a tuned convex combination of normalized scores can beat RRF within a single domain
The HNSW citation (arXiv:1603.09320, Malkov and Yashunin) checks out exactly, and the algorithm is indeed the one underlying pgvector's HNSW index
Contextual embeddings alone cut top-20-chunk retrieval failures 35%, stacking contextual BM25 gets to 49%, and adding a final reranking pass gets to 67%
Li et al.'s Search-o1 (arXiv:2501.05366) genuinely proposes an agentic RAG loop plus a document-distillation module for reasoning models
accuracy on 20-document multi-document QA falls from roughly 75% when the answer sits first, to around 55% when it's buried in the middle, before recovering to about 72% when it's last
nothing in Liu et al.'s experiments tests or recommends keep the top 10
CocoIndex is real — an Apache-2.0, Rust-core project with 10.9k GitHub stars, 833 forks, and 211 releases
CocoIndex's own enterprise marketing page lists no named customers or case studies
the flashiest specific claim language-aware chunking turns out to be a custom regex-boundary splitter the company built itself, not the Tree-sitter/AST-based chunker CocoIndex advertises
A vendor benchmark from Tiger Data reported 471.57 QPS at 99% recall on 50 million 768-dimension vectors, versus 41.47 QPS for Qdrant
Confident AI and Firecrawl reportedly moved workloads from Pinecone to Postgres/pgvector, citing roughly $250/month versus $675/month at 10 million vectors
MCP SDK was reportedly seeing roughly 97 million monthly downloads, up from about 100,000 in its first month

in December 2025 Anthropic donated protocol governance to a new Agentic AI Foundation under the Linux Foundation, with AWS, Anthropic, Block, Bloomberg, Cloudflare, Google, Microsoft, and OpenAI all seated as Platinum members
Early versions of Claude Code used RAG + a local vector db, but we found pretty quickly that agentic search generally works better
intent to adopt hybrid retrieval architectures roughly tripled from 10.3% to 33.3% between January and March 2026
Glean priced a $150M Series F at a $7.2B valuation in June 2025 — up from $4.6B nine months earlier
ARR crossing $200M by December 2025 and roughly $300M by May 2026, tripling in fifteen months
the most detailed public description of Glean's retrieval stack traces back through several layers of secondary blog synthesis to a single April 2023 podcast conversation
reported initial contract values run $100K–$500K annually for organizations above roughly 500 employees, with Fortune 500 deals clearing $5M
vendor-partner enterprise AI deployments succeed roughly twice as often as in-house builds (67% versus 33%), drawn from an MIT GenAI Divide study
Onyx reports usage at Ramp (115,000 queries/month), Thales (1,400 monthly active users across 82,000 employees), and an air-gapped deployment at UC San Diego
Cerebras priced its IPO at $185 a share on May 14, 2026, opened at $385, and closed its debut day at $311
a post-IPO low of $160.81 on June 26, after CEO Andrew Feldman said the company's margin forecast had been misunderstood
GAAP revenue of $193.4 million, up 94% year over year, against a GAAP operating loss of $15.0 million and gross margin near 45%
86% of 2025 revenue came from two UAE-linked entities, G42 (24%) and Mohamed bin Zayed University of AI (62%)
SambaNova landed a $1B Series F at an $11B valuation on July 8, 2026, alongside a marquee win naming SambaNova as JPMorgan Chase's on-prem inference provider
third-party workforce tracking shows growth from roughly 358 employees in January 2024 to about 1,016 by December 2025
an open Developer Experience Engineer role explicitly described in its own listing as the company's engine for visibility and growth
no dedicated Hacker News discussion thread for How We Built Our Knowledge Base could be found
